title: campanula afra var. pallida
intro: Campanula afra var. pallida, also known as the White Harebell, is a flowering plant species within the Campanula family, characterized by its pale blue to white bell-shaped flowers and silver-gray foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of glycosides and flavonoids. The health benefits of Campanula afra var. pallida include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to fully confirm its efficacy. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried for use in capsules or topically applied as a cream or ointment.
